elements.description: A second stair callout 'ST2 A-19 บันได ดูแบบขยาย' is printed near the top-left of the sheet, close to ซักล้าง -- but no distinct stair icon (step lines) is visibly drawn at that location on this ground-floor plan the way the ST3 main stair's steps are drawn in โถง, or the way the ST1 wedge is drawn south of grid A. Not confidently resolved whether ST2 refers to a small utility/service stair not clearly rendered at this scale, a callout that actually belongs to the upper floor plan (หน้า07) and is only printed here for reference, or a labeling inconsistency.

Numerous small triangle symbols (▷◁▽△, some containing a digit '1') are scattered around the room perimeters, plus circled and boxed numerals per the symbol legend (หน้า03). The bracket number in each room's box (e.g. '3 +0.50 2') is a floor-material category (matches the A-01 material list พื้น items 1-5), the trailing circled/boxed number is the door/window opening reference. Individual triangle-symbol meaning (door swing direction vs a separate vent/reference marker) and exact circle-vs-box shape are not reliably distinguishable at this resolution for every tag.

warnings: This is the ground-floor plan sheet (A-04, แปลนพื้นชั้น 1) of a 2-story house -- the roof plan is on its own separate sheet (A-06, หน้า08), and the 2nd floor is on its own separate sheet (A-05, หน้า07, see sibling _หน้า07_floor_plan_upper.json).

GRID-RELEVANT DUMMY-GRID RESOLUTION (reporting only, master file not edited) -- THIS IS THE KEY CROSS-CHECK REQUESTED: the grid master (หน้า00_gridline.json) flags a provisional dummy grid line 'Aprime' at pos_m=-2.05 (south of named grid A), sourced from real beams B1/B2 on the structural floor-1 beam plan (S-04) spanning grid1-grid2, described there only speculatively as 'a possible carport/terrace extension.' This architecture ground-floor plan (A-04) resolves it conclusively: the left-side dimension chain explicitly prints '2.05' as a distinct dimension below grid row A, and at that exact 2.05m offset sits a small triangular/wedge-shaped room labeled 'บันได ST1 A-18 ดูแบบขยาย ±0.00' (floor material 4) -- i.e. a front ENTRANCE STAIR/STEP (ST1, detailed on sheet A-18), not a carport or open terrace as the structural side speculated. The x-position also matches (grid1-grid2 span, under ทางเดิน). RECOMMENDATION: update the grid master's 'Aprime' note to reflect that this dummy grid line corresponds to the entrance-stair (ST1) landing, not a carport/terrace slab -- flagged here rather than edited directly since the master gridline file was not to be touched by this extraction pass.

This house has 3 distinct stair-related callouts printed on the ground floor: ST1 (A-18, the small entrance-step wedge 2.05m south of grid A), ST3 (A-20, the main switchback staircase in โถง, unambiguously drawn with steps 1-8), and ST2 (A-19, a callout printed near ซักล้าง with NO clearly matching drawn stair icon at this scale) -- see the 'stair_ST2_callout_unlocated' element above, flagged for human review, similar in spirit to house #4's dual ST2/ST3 callout ambiguity on its own ground floor (though there the ambiguity was 2 tags on ONE icon; here it is 1 tag with NO visibly matching icon).

Individual door/window counts and the exact triangle-symbol convention used on this sheet (vs. the circle/hexagon convention documented on the symbol legend, หน้า03) were not confidently resolvable at this image resolution -- flagged for zoom review rather than guessed.

Elevation-direction callout bubble (bottom of sheet: 3/A-09 top, 2/A-08 left, 4/A-10 right, 1/A-07 bottom) and section-cut callout arrows (tags '1/A-11' near top-left, '2/A-12' appearing twice on the left and right sides) are printed on this sheet -- cross-referenced when extracting those sheets, not re-itemized as elements here. Note this house has only 2 sections (A-11, A-12) vs house #4's 3 (A-11/A-12/A-13).
WC1 is detailed on its own dedicated 1:25-scale sheet (A-15, หน้า17 per this house's index) -- this floor-plan-level box is superseded by that sheet for exact fixture layout.

Top dimension chain (0.60 west overhang | 1.50+0.50+2.00=4.00 (grid1-2) | 0.30+2.70=3.00 (grid3-4 sub-split) | 0.50 east overhang) and left dimension chain (1.00 north overhang | 2.00(F-E) | 3.70(E-D, sub 0.45+0.55 partial) | 2.20(D-C, sub 0.55+0.35 partial) | 3.70(C-B) | 1.80(B-A, sub 0.60+0.50 partial) | 2.05 south of A) both match this house's grid master (x: 1=0,2=4,3=6,4=9; y: F=13.40,E=11.40,D=7.70,C=5.50,B=1.80,A=0) exactly for the main grid spacing.
